content: Just as the linguistic sense of reflexion (meaning reflection as both a cognitive practice and a mirrored optical effect) teaches learners to reframe existing knowledge by stepping back to examine their own writing, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by drawing on high school language arts pedagogy that structures reflexion as a guided step-by-step practice rather than an ad-hoc agent check. Specifically, requiring LLMs to articulate mismatches between their intended output and the expected response (similar to how students are taught to revise essays by reflecting on feedback) can amplify the performance gains of existing multi-perspective self-reflection techniques.
